title: TLS, Virtual Hosts, Credentials, and Authorization · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A Go pairing room incrementally implements a Go TLS and least-privilege connection contract, first with deterministic values and then with a changed failure, while keeping prior messaging invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go TLS and least-privilege connection contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Amqps Identity Verification
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 3169 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify TLS and vhost identity, redact secrets, and compute least-privilege permissions; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Amqps Identity Verification.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use tls with trusted ca, hostname and sni verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Amqps Identity Verification, keep Amqps Identity Verification connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Audit the initial evidence packet · Workshop Tenant Isolation Abuse
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 1550 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify TLS and vhost identity, redact secrets, and compute least-privilege permissions; reject stale connection and channel generations; then reuse a message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
